slides.title: People in Society
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 4 English, Unit 6: People in Society. Welcome to Unit 6! This unit is all about the people in our society — the many workers, leaders, and helpers who make a community work. You will meet engineers and carpenters, governors and senators, artists and photographers, and you will learn about refugees, immigrants, and the roles we all play. Along the way you will use the Present Simple to describe jobs, should and mustn't to give advice and rules, and question words to interview people about their work. By the end, you will be able to describe the helpers in your own town — just like Sarah does in this unit's story, “The Community Parade”. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name and describe at least twenty jobs and roles that people play in society
Explain what different workers and leaders do, using the Present Simple
Give advice and state rules using should and mustn't
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name and describe at least twenty jobs and roles that people play in society. you will also explain what different workers and leaders do, using the present simple. you will also give advice and state rules using should and mustn't. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: janitor, carpenter, laborer, engineer, merchant, governor. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Two Neighbours: A Refugee and an Immigrant
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes The People of Our Town and Two Neighbours: A Refugee and an Immigrant. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Present Simple
bullets: We use the Present Simple to talk about routines, facts, and things that happen again and again. It is perfect for describing jobs, because most jobs are...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Present Simple. We use the Present Simple to talk about routines, facts, and things that happen again and again. It is perfect for describing jobs, because most jobs are the same every day: “The janitor cleans the school every morning.” Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Describe a Job. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
